title: Repetition Code Simulator
description: Simulate a simple repetition code to see how syndrome extraction can detect some errors without directly measuring the logical information itself.
whyItMatters: Quantum error correction is easy to praise and hard to picture. A repetition-code simulator makes the logic of redundancy and syndrome measurement concrete.

steps.title: Define a simple noise model
steps.description: Introduce one or two basic error channels such as bit-flip or phase-flip noise.
steps.tips: Keep the first version simple enough that you can explain every outcome.

steps.title: Encode a logical state
steps.description: Spread the information across several physical qubits using a repetition-code pattern.
steps.tips: Make clear what the logical information is before noise is added.

steps.title: Extract syndrome information
steps.description: Measure parity-style checks that reveal whether an error happened without reading out the logical value directly.
steps.tips: That separation is the heart of the concept.

steps.title: Attempt correction
steps.description: Apply a basic recovery rule and compare corrected versus uncorrected outcomes.
steps.tips: Even a simple decoder teaches useful lessons about trade-offs.

steps.title: Summarise the limits
steps.description: Explain what the code can and cannot protect against and why scaling is hard.
steps.tips: The limits matter as much as the success case.

deliverables: Short explanation of syndrome logic and limitations

rubric.description: The simulator captures the difference between data and syndrome information.
The learner understands what the code protects against and what it does not.
The results make the error-correction logic visible rather than opaque.
